title: Jan 2024 thoughts — grey-hair anxiety, the Paige situation, attractive-Henry dread
html:
Jan 2024 thoughts — grey-hair anxiety, the Paige situation, attractive-Henry dread

Source: 2024-01-05 Google Doc, 12:46 AM. Day-2 of the late-night-cry pair (continuing from 2024 01 04 thoughts on 1323 dad in bad state). Three completely different anxieties in one entry: vanity (grey hair), ethics (the Paige incident), sexuality (Henry's attractiveness).

TL;DR

  • Grey-hair anxiety: ongoing self-conscious fear about random stray grey hairs at the back of his head. Walks self-consciously to avoid people seeing the back of his head. "There is nothing you can do about it except for breathe and move on... but that rarely seems to work." Logically dismisses it (genetics + dye option) but the discomfort persists.
  • The Paige situation (the bigger ethical knot):
    • Yahir was extremely drunk to near-blackout, in a room with Paige.
    • He made a sexual move; faint memory of Paige saying "no yg don't do that."
    • Paige's account: he attempted a "light sexual choke."
    • Riley + Cameron walked in on the situation; their read was that Paige was reciprocating ("she was above me").
    • Owen also believes Paige was opportunistically engaging.
    • Yahir didn't remember any of it until Riley brought it up at Sam's Club breakfast.
    • The decision question: should Yahir tell Sam (his current thing, also Paige's roommate + best friend)?
    • Yahir's reasoning: telling shines bad light on Paige; not telling = evading truth that may explode later; ultimately "If Sam and I do start to become something more than just party fuck around buddies, then I'll have to tell her."
    • Self-judgment (the most cutting line): "that is disgustingly predatorial and kill yourself before you become a sexual slob while drunk. Its genuinely fucking pathetic when you go for pussy when you have to consciousness and reality to do it in. Your default is chilling and vibing while drunk, not sexual orientation."
  • The Henry-attractiveness dread:
    • Luke + Henry coming back; Yahir is anxious about Henry's attractiveness.
    • "Almost like I don't want him to come in the scenario that I do some regrettable bisexual shit when he's around."
    • Self-reassurance (similar pattern to grey hairs): "I am sexually attracted and emotionally attracted to women. I have never been aroused by a man... but I can appreciate when a man is an attractive entity."
    • The earlier Gabe interaction referenced parenthetically as the only weird memory.
    • Resolution: "You are not gay, you are not bisexual, you simply get weirded out and feeling off-kilter when you find another man attractive."

Pattern observed

Three anxieties on three different axes — somatic (hair), ethical (drunk consent crisis), sexual (gender/attraction uncertainty) — all processed via the same anxiety-then-rational-self-reassurance template:

  1. Notice the worry.
  2. Dismiss it logically.
  3. Note that dismissal doesn't actually relieve the feeling.
  4. Re-state the rational position.
  5. Move on (uneasily).

The Paige-situation processing is the most ethically fraught block in the corpus. Yahir's self-talk "kill yourself before you become a sexual slob while drunk" is the harshest internal language documented anywhere. This is a real consent-grey-zone incident with multiple witnesses, a victim's account, and Yahir's blackout — and his processing is to not tell Sam, with a contingent "if it gets serious" clause.

The Henry attractiveness dread is the only sexuality-self-questioning content in the corpus. Yahir lands on a "appreciating attractiveness ≠ being attracted" frame; whether that's true self-knowledge or a defense isn't determinable from this note alone.

This pair of late-Jan-2024 notes (2024 01 04 thoughts on 1323 dad in bad state + this one) is Yahir at his most exposed in writing.

Open questions

  • Did the Paige situation ever come out to Sam? (Per the April 2024 Sam breakup: not mentioned — Sam's lie about Jaden was the breakup trigger, not a Paige reveal.)
  • Is "Henry" the same Henry from the boys' lineup?
  • The Gabe interaction reference — what was it? (Single mention.)
  • Did Riley + Cameron + Owen ever talk to Paige directly?
